title: Controller (US e-commerce)
description:
Title: Controller



Looking for a disciplined financial owner who brings calm to complexity 



The Controller role at FS is for someone who enjoys applying knowledge, logic, and structure to create reliable financial systems. This is not a CFO or advisory role. It is a role for a financial professional who takes ownership of execution and finds satisfaction in predictable, trusted outcomes. 



When this role is done well, leadership stops double-checking numbers, inventory decisions are disciplined, and finance becomes boring in the best possible way. 



If you prefer facts over narratives, systems over heroics, and clarity over urgency, this role is built for you.



The mission 

Your mission is to own the financial operating system. You ensure financials are accurate, 

timely, and decision-ready, with clear cash visibility and enforced controls, without requiring 

constant leadership involvement. 



What you will do 

? Own monthly close and deliver accurate financials on a consistent schedule. 

? Ensure all accounts are reconciled and audit-ready. 

? Maintain clear tracking of owner expenses. 

? Prepare board-ready financial summaries with variances and cash implications. 

? Build and maintain a rolling 13-week cash flow forecast. 

? Surface liquidity risks and tradeoffs early. 

? Own inventory finance and open-to-buy controls. 

? Review open-to-buy monthly with Supply Chain and approve or block purchasing. 

? Design and enforce financial controls and approval workflows. 

? Own AR and AP systems and performance. 

? Direct and review the work of the Accountant. 

? Improve AR procedures to support cash timing. 

? Coordinate sales tax obligations and R&D tax tracking. 

? Audit key vendor invoices and charges as needed. 



What success looks like 

? Financials are delivered on time, every month. 

? Cash visibility is clear, current, and trusted. 

? Inventory decisions reflect cash discipline. 

? Controls prevent errors and surprises. 

? Finance operates calmly and predictably. 

? Leadership trusts the numbers without rechecking. 



This role is for you if 

? You enjoy structured, analytical work. 

? You prefer enforcing standards over negotiating them. 

? You like applying proven methods rather than inventing new ones. 

? You are comfortable owning deadlines and saying no when needed. 

? You communicate in a direct, factual way. 



This role is not for you if 

? You want a strategy-first or advisory role. 

? You dislike routine or repetition. 

? You prefer autonomy without guardrails. 

? You thrive on urgency, ambiguity, or constant change.



To Apply: send cv/resume and answers to the ff questions below:

1) Do you have 5+ years of hands-on accounting/controller experience owning the monthly close process?

If yes, briefly explain what you personally owned end-to-end.



2) Have you worked in a product-based business such as e-commerce, retail, wholesale, inventory-heavy, or consumer goods? If yes, name the company/industry and describe the finance work you handled.



3) Do you have experience with inventory finance, open-to-buy planning, or approving/blocking purchasing decisions based on cash and inventory position?



4) Have you led, reviewed, or managed the work of an Accountant or junior accounting staff? If yes, how many people did you oversee, and what work did you review?



5) Have you worked with U.S. accounting operations, including areas such as sales tax, accruals, month-end close, board reporting, or cash planning? If yes, explain which areas you handled.



6) Are you fully available to work CST hours for this role?

title: Lead Accountant (E-Commerce / Consumer Products)
description:
Location: Remote (Offshore)

Working Hours: Aligned with Central U.S. Time

Compensation: $3,000/month



Overview

 We're a US based pet nutrition and supplement company based in St. Louis, Missouri, founded in 2017 focused on high-quality pet supplements, built around trust, transparency, and product integrity.



This role is not a support function. It is a leadership position within the offshore accounting team.

The Lead Accountant will take full ownership of core accounting operations, oversee a small team, and ensure financial accuracy across the business. This role requires someone who can operate independently, make sound decisions, and maintain a high standard without constant oversight.



Key Responsibilities

>Oversee and supervise Accounts Receivable (AR) and Accounts Payable (AP) processes executed by staff

>Review and approve outgoing payments

>Perform and review bank reconciliations

>Prepare monthly journal entries and ensure accuracy

>Lead the month-end close process and ensure timelines are met

>Prepare audit support and documentation as required

>Review financials and journal entries for completeness and accuracy

>Own revenue reconciliations, including:

1) Retailer-specific rebates

2) Deduction mapping and reconciliation

>Identify gaps, improve processes, and maintain strong internal controls



Team Leadership

>Directly manage and support 2 offshore accounting staff

>Provide guidance, review work, and maintain accountability

>Set standards for accuracy, timeliness, and communication



What Success Looks Like

Month-end close is completed on time, every time, without follow-ups

Financials are clean, accurate, and require minimal revisions

AR and AP workflows run smoothly without bottlenecks

Revenue reconciliations are precise, with clear handling of rebates and deductions

Audit requests are fulfilled quickly with well-organised documentation

The offshore team operates with accountability and consistent output quality

Leadership does not need to chase updates or double-check core work



Ideal Candidate Profile

>Years of experience in accounting, with exposure to ecommerce or consumer products

>Experience leading or mentoring junior accountants

>Strong grasp of revenue reconciliation, including deductions and rebates

>Comfortable owning the month-end close process end-to-end

>Detail-oriented to a fault, with strong analytical thinking

>Direct communicator who can push back when needed

>Must be able to work Central U.S. hours consistently



To Apply: Please send your resume/cv with answers to the ff screening questions below:



1) Do you have previous accounting experience in an e-commerce, retail, or consumer products company?

If yes, please share the company names, industry, and the accounting work you personally handled.



2) Have you worked with U.S.-based clients or companies before?

If yes, please explain the type of work you handled, who you reported to, and how closely you worked with U.S. stakeholders.



3) Have you led, reviewed, or mentored accountants or junior accounting staff? If yes, how many people did you oversee, and what exactly did you review or coach them on?



4) Do you have hands-on experience with revenue reconciliation?

If yes, please explain the types of reconciliations you handled, including any work involving deductions, rebates, chargebacks, or retailer-related adjustments.



5) Are you comfortable working night shift aligned with Central U.S. hours on a full-time basis?



6) Have you personally owned or heavily supported month-end close? If yes, walk us through the parts of the close process you handled directly.



7) What accounting systems and tools have you used most recently?

title: Senior Accountant (Real Estate)
description:
Schedule: Full CST hours 

Salary: $1,800-$2,000+ USD/mon

*basically a Sr Accountant or Controller level individual contributor (not manager)

*can do all hands on work but also prep financial statements, AP/AR, monthly closing, all reports



Position Summary

We are seeking a highly capable and strategic financial operator to serve as a senior-level bookkeeper/controller for our fast-paced, multi-entity real estate enterprise. 



This role supports our real estate brokerage team, investment and development projects, and the personal financial operations of the CEO. The ideal candidate combines technical accounting expertise with strategic insight and the ability to manage complex, multi-entity structures in a lean, high-performance environment.



Key Responsibilities

- Maintain full-cycle bookkeeping for brokerage, investment entities, and project-specific LLCs.

- Oversee job cost accounting, project-level reporting, and GAAP-compliant multi-entity financials.

- Lead monthly closes, consolidate reports, and provide variance analysis.

- Perform weekly/monthly reconciliations of all accounts and ensure books are audit-ready.

- Maintain documentation and prepare reports for CPA coordination and tax filings.

- Ensure accurate 1099 issuance and vendor W-9 compliance.

- Build rolling cash flow forecasts and participate in capital allocation decisions.

- Manage the CEO’s personal finances, including financial tracking, reporting, and investment oversight.

- Support quarterly strategy sessions with data and insight, not just numbers.



Qualifications

- Bachelor's degree in Accounting or Finance; CPA or CMA preferred.

- 5+ years experience in bookkeeping or financial management, ideally in real estate.

- Strong command of US GAAP, project accounting, and inter-entity financial structures.

- Proficiency in QuickBooks, project tracking systems, and financial dashboards.

- Strong analytical thinking, attention to detail, and communication skills.

- Experience managing personal financials for executives a strong plus.



Ideal Candidate Traits

- Strategic thinker who loves both numbers and their meaning.

- Exceptionally organized and accountable.

- Proactive problem-solver who can navigate lean operations.

- Deeply reliable and detail-oriented with a high sense of ownership.

- Comfortable with both the tactical and strategic aspects of finance.



Compensation & Benefits

- Competitive salary based on experience and qualifications.

- Flexible work environment and working remotely.

- Performance-based incentives and potential for expanded leadership role.

title: E-commerce Marketing Coordinator (Full-time)
description:
Location: Remote

Department: Marketing / E-commerce

Reports to: Marketing Manager

Schedule: Full CST hours 



Why This Role Exists

This role exists to own day-to-day e-commerce execution so launches, promotions, and PDP updates don’t bottleneck at the Marketing Manager level.

The E-commerce Marketing Associate consolidates Shopify execution, launch coordination, and marketing operations into a single accountable role. As launch volume increases (3–4/month), this role improves speed, accuracy, and execution quality across PDPs, promotions, and site updates.

You’ll act as the execution right-hand to the Marketing Manager — owning backend setup, QA, CRO execution, and cross-functional coordination, while surfacing risks early and keeping work moving forward.



Role Scorecard & Outcomes

E-commerce Execution & Site Ownership

Shopify backend is clean, accurate, and fully maintained. PDPs, collections, promotions, and homepage updates are executed correctly and on time with minimal supervision.

Launch Readiness & QA

All launches are fully set up, QA’d, and executed without last-minute issues or escalations.

Conversion & CRO Support

PDP and site improvements are consistently implemented, contributing to improved CVR, engagement, or UX clarity over baseline.

Inventory Awareness & Readiness

Inventory status is clearly understood from a marketing perspective. OOS risks, restocks, and back-in-stock opportunities are proactively flagged and supported through site updates and campaign readiness.

Operational Reliability

Reporting, inventory visibility, and promo-related site updates are accurate, timely, and dependable.

Cross-Functional Coordination

Designers, copywriters, inventory, and agencies receive clear briefs, timelines, and follow-ups. Work progresses without repeated intervention from the Marketing Manager.



Key Responsibilities

E-commerce & Website Execution

Build and manage PDPs in the Shopify backend (SKUs, variants, pricing, bundles, collections).

Implement PDP copy (from Copywriter) and visual assets (from Designer).

Execute homepage, collection, and promotional site updates.

Maintain site hygiene: broken links, formatting issues, outdated content, and UX friction.

QA the site prior to launches to ensure accuracy, functionality, and readiness.

Integrate SEO content into the website.

Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO)

Support CRO initiatives including layout adjustments, upsells, trust elements, and merchandising improvements.

Execute approved A/B tests and experiments; document changes and results.

Product Launch Execution

Coordinate product launches and promotions based on the marketing calendar.

Own backend launch setup end-to-end (products, pricing, promos, collections, inventory sync).

Conduct basic competitor pricing analysis and margin checks when required.

Support post-launch performance summaries and insights.

Marketing Operations

Maintain daily, weekly, and monthly scorecards.

Monitor inventory levels relevant to marketing execution (OOS, low stock, restocks, incoming inventory).

Partner with Inventory and Operations teams to stay aligned on availability, timing, and constraints.

Support back-in-stock, relaunch, and inventory-driven campaigns through site and launch readiness.

Coordinate with internal teams and external agencies to keep timelines on track.

Flag execution risks, blockers, or dependencies early — with proposed solutions.



Ways of Working & Mindset

Ownership mindset: Takes responsibility for outcomes, not just tasks.

Bias toward action: Moves work forward with imperfect information.

Commercially aware: Understands how inventory, timing, and execution impact revenue.

Detail-obsessed but pragmatic: High accuracy without paralysis.

Solution-oriented: Comes with options, not just problems.

Calm under pressure: Handles launch changes without noise.

Right-hand mentality: Thinks one step ahead to reduce load on the Marketing Manager.





Core Competencies

Strong Shopify and e-commerce backend fluency.

High attention to detail and execution accuracy.

CRO and merchandising fundamentals.

Inventory awareness for e-commerce and lifecycle marketing.

Clear written communication and coordination skills.

Comfort working with performance data and reporting.



Candidate Must-Haves

Proven experience executing e-commerce updates in Shopify or similar platforms.

Strong analytical literacy using GA4, Shopify Analytics, and/or heat map tools (Hotjar, Clarity).

Ability to manage multiple execution tasks with speed and accuracy.

Strong problem-solving instincts and independent judgment.

Excellent QA discipline, especially around launches, pricing, promos, and inventory-driven updates.

title: Experienced Associate Brand Manager to support 12+ Amazon brands for growth, execution, drive performance and operation
description:
Role Overview



We're hiring 2 Associate Brand Manager who will work directly under the Senior Brand Manager to execute day-to-day brand operations. This is a hands-on role focused on the “legwork” that drives performance: optimizing listings, managing keyword research, coordinating creative projects, and pulling actionable data.



If the Senior Brand Manager sets the playbook, the Associate Brand Manager runs the plays — ensuring everything gets executed on time, correctly, and at a high level.



Key Responsibilities

1. Listing Management & Optimization

Create, update, and fully optimize Amazon product listings (titles, bullets, descriptions, images, videos, A+ content).

Conduct keyword research and competitor analysis to improve rankings and visibility.

Ensure all listings remain Rufus and Cosmos compliant with Amazon’s latest requirements.

Test content variations (A/B testing) to improve CTR and conversions.



2. Data Research & Analysis

Pull performance data from Amazon Seller Central and external tools (Data Dive, Helium 10, Jungle Scout).

Identify trends, keyword shifts, and competitor activity.

Provide actionable recommendations to the Senior Brand Manager to improve conversions, CTR, and sales velocity.



3. Cross-Team Collaboration

Work closely with the graphic design team to request, coordinate, and deliver images, infographics, and A+ creatives.

Partner with the marketing team to support product launches, promotions, and review-generation initiatives.

Ensure all projects are aligned with deadlines, strategy, and brand guidelines.



4. Conversion-Focused Execution

Continuously update and refine listings to improve conversion rates and customer engagement.

Maintain Amazon Brand Stores, A+ content, and ad creatives for consistency and performance.

Assist in preparing assets for influencer campaigns and external marketing pushes.



5. Project & Compliance Support

Own the administrative “heavy lifting” of flat files, bulk uploads, and catalog troubleshooting.

Flag compliance issues, unauthorized sellers, or listing errors for quick resolution.

Support market expansion efforts by preparing research and draft listings for new product opportunities.



Skills & Qualifications



2+ years experience in Amazon FBA operations, brand management, or digital marketing.

Proficient in Amazon Seller Central, flat files, listing creation, and troubleshooting.

Strong skills in SEO, keyword research, and listing optimization.

Data-driven mindset; confident in Google Sheets and reporting.

Strong communication skills to work cross-functionally with design, marketing, and operations teams.

Highly organized, detail-oriented, and capable of managing multiple deadlines.



What Success Looks Like

Every listing across assigned brands is fully optimized, compliant, and performing.

Senior Brand Manager receives clear, accurate reports and recommendations.

Creative assets are delivered on time because you coordinated smoothly with design.



Product launches happen seamlessly with marketing and review strategies in place.

Conversions and rankings consistently improve due to your execution. If you feel you are the right fit, please respond to the job with the title " The Best Supporting Branding Manager Out There!"



Please respond directly to :

----------



Kind Regards,

Tam Ma

Co Founder

Prodigy Media

title: Marketing Automation Specialist
description:
ABOUT US

Amplifly Studio is a boutique AI-powered brand amplification studio based in New Zealand. We work with a focused client roster — property developers, ecommerce brands, and growth-stage businesses — handling brand strategy, social media, paid media, content creation,
 marketing, and marketing automation.

We are lean by design. Our model is founder-directed, contractor-executed. That means every person on our team does real work, has real ownership of their domain, and communicates directly with the founder. No layers.

We are early-stage and building fast. The systems you build here will become the replicable backbone of how we service every client we onboard.



THE ROLE

We need a Marketing Automation Specialist who can own the technical build of our automation and data infrastructure — across our own agency operations and all active client accounts.

This is not a strategy role. We need someone who can build, wire, test, and document. You will take defined briefs and Notion specs and turn them into working 
 scenarios, Airtable bases, and connected marketing stack flows. WHAT YOU WILL BUILD

Your initial build list spans three areas. These will be scoped and briefed individually — this post gives you the full picture so you can assess fit.



DATA + AIRTABLE INFRASTRUCTURE

– Build and maintain Airtable bases that auto-populate from upstream triggers (HubSpot, Make, webhooks)

– Connect intake forms, CRM updates, and proposal status changes to Airtable records

– Structure bases as reusable templates the agency can clone per new client



CONTENT + CREATIVE PIPELINE

– Build the content creation loop: brief generation ? asset creation triggers ? scheduling ? performance data loopback

– Connect AI image generation and creative tools into Make flows

– Automate posting workflows across Publer and Buffer from Airtable-sourced content calendars

– Route performance data fro
ta, Google, and social platforms back into reporting dashboards



MARKETING STACK AUTOMATIONS

– Social listening: set up monitoring flows for brand mentions, competitor activity, and keyword tracking

– Competitor site scraping: structured data capture from defined competitor URLs on a scheduled basis

– SEO automation: rank tracking pipelines, crawl scheduling, and data routing to reporting

– Google Ads and Meta Ads: connect campaign data into centralised dashboards; automate reporting pulls

– Shopify (Happy Valley Honey): order data, product feed, abandoned cart, and inventory automation flows

–
 automation: Make + HubSpot/Klaviyo flows for triggered sequences and list hygiene

– Agency operations: pitch-to-payment pipeline automation (HubSpot ? Airtable ? Make ? PandaDoc ? Xero)



WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT

– You will not write brand strategy or creative briefs — that is the founder's domain

– You will not manage social media accounts or write captions

– You will not build the Taylored Group customer care portal — that is a separate hire and workstream

– You will not manage paid media campaigns — you will connect and automate the data flows around them



SKILLS REQUIRED

Non-negotiable:

– 
 (formerly Integromat) — you must be proficient, not learning

– Airtable — relational base design, automations, views, and API connections

– Webhooks, APIs, and JSON — comfortable reading and working with API responses

– Google Sheets / data routing — pulling platform data into structured outputs

– Shopify — understanding of the data model and available integrations



Strong advantage:

– HubSpot — workflows, properties, pipeline automation

– Meta Ads and Google Ads data connections (not campaign management)

– Web scraping tools (Apify, Browse AI, or similar)

– Social listening tool setup (Brand24, Mention, or similar)

– SEO tool APIs (Ahrefs, Semrush data routing)

– Klaviyo automation flows

– PandaDoc variable fields and webhook triggers

– Xero API basics



Not required:

– Design skills — we have that covered

– Paid media management experience — we manage campaigns separately

– NZ-based — timezone overlap (NZ or AEST) is a strong preference, not a requirement



HOW WE WORK

You report directly to the founder. There is no middle layer.

Work is briefed in Notion. Every build has a spec before it goes to you. You are expected to flag gaps in the brief before you build, not after.

We use Loom for async video reviews. Most communication is async. We have a weekly check-in.

Documentation is not optional. Every scenario and flow you build must be documented in Notion before it is considered complete. We build for replicability — your documentation is part of the deliverable.



HOW TO APPLY

Send us a short cover note — not a template, not a form letter. Tell us:

– Which part of this scope you are most confident in, and where you would want support

– One automation build you are proud of — what the problem was, what you built, and what the outcome was

– Your hourly rate in USD

– Confirm you can work with ah NZTimezone



We will review applications as they come in and move quickly for the right person.

title: Video Editor – Replace Audio in Multiple Languages (Simple, High-Volume, Long-Term Work)
description:
**Before applying, read everything carefully.**



We are hiring a team to handle a simple but high-volume workflow. We operate 3 main YouTube channels (franchises), and each one must be translated into 24 additional languages. This means you will take 15 original videos per channel per month and adapt them into multiple languages, resulting in a total of 1080 videos per month across all channels. Your role is to replace the original English audio with the provided voiceovers, then upload and schedule the videos. Everything is already prepared—no translation, scripting, or creative editing is required. The work is repetitive, fast and requires consistency, speed, and strong communication.



We are looking for an existing team of at least 3 people (more is better) for a full-time, long-term position within our company. This is a stable opportunity with potential for growth over time.



### Job Description



The work is very simple and highly repetitive:



* You will receive long-form YouTube videos in English.

* We provide all translated voiceovers in other 24 languages.

* Your task is to **remove the original English audio and replace it with the provided voiceover**.

* You may need to slightly adjust timing (speed up/slow down), which we will teach you.

* Once done, you must **upload and schedule the videos on YouTube** (full training provided).



The work is easy but requires consistency, speed, and discipline.



### Work Volume



* We have **3 main English channels**

* Each channel produces **15 original videos per month** So, 45 videos a month in total.

* Each video must be adapted into **24 additional languages**



**Important:** We provide all voiceovers. Your role is simply to replace the audio—no translation or content creation required.



This means:



* Each original channel (3 total) expands into 24 translated channels

* In total, this results in **75 channels overall (3 original + 72 translated channels)**

* **360 videos per channel per month** (15 videos × 24 languages)

* Across all channels: **1080 videos per month** (360 videos x 3 channels)



Fast execution and clear communication are essential.



### Requirements



* **Minimum team of 3 people (required from the start)**

* Full-time availability (**8+ hours per day / 7 days a week**)

* Strong work ethic and commitment from day one

* Ability to meet deadlines consistently

* Experience with YouTube workflows

* Willingness to learn quickly and follow systems



If standards are not met, collaboration will end immediately.



### Payment



* **$120 per channel**

* Total: **$360 for all 3 channels**

Monthly payment, always on time.



### What We Offer



* Long-term, stable work

* Fully built and optimized system

* Step-by-step training

* Dedicated manager to support you

* Opportunity to grow within the team



### How to Apply



To apply, you must send ALL of the following:



1. A photo of your setup (workspace)

2. Full computer specifications (CPU, GPU, etc.)

3. Internet speed test (WiFi)

4. Answer this question:



   * What is your favorite YouTube channel and why?



Also explain **why you are interested in this job**.



**If any of these are missing, you will not receive a response.**



### Important



We are looking for people who are serious, fast, and ready to work hard. There is high demand for this role, and we will select the team that shows the strongest commitment.

title: Graphic Designer (Marketing & Ad Creatives) – Long-Term Opportunity
description:
Graphic Designer (Marketing & Ad Creatives) – Long-Term Opportunity



We’re looking for a graphic designer who can create clean, modern, and effective visuals for digital marketing.



Our team works with businesses across the U.S. and Canada, building marketing systems that include ads, funnels, and automation. Design plays a key role in how these systems perform, and we’re looking for someone who understands both creativity and clarity.



This is a long-term opportunity for someone who wants consistent work, growth, and to be part of a fast-moving team.



What You’ll Be Doing:

- Design ad creatives for platforms like
 and Instagram

- Create visuals for landing pages and funnels

- Produce clean, modern graphics aligned with brand and messaging

- Develop multiple variations of designs for testing and performance

- Support with banners, thumbnails, and other digital assets



You’ll work closely with our marketing and video teams to bring ideas to life visually.



What We’re Looking For:

- Strong design skills with a clean, modern style

- Experience creating ad creatives for digital marketing campaigns

- Good sense of layout, hierarchy, and visual clarity

- Strong attention to detail

- Ability to follow direction and also contribute ideas

- Reliable, efficient, and able to meet deadlines

- Good English communication

- Experience designing ad creatives that are meant to convert

- Understanding of marketing or performance-based design

- Ability to follow brand guidelines and maintain consistency across designs



Bonus to Have:

- Experience working with video editors or marketing teams

- Basic motion or animation skills



Important:

This is not a beginner role.

We are looking for someone who is comfortable working independently and can produce high-quality work without step-by-step guidance.

You should understand that design is not just about visuals — it’s about creating assets that support performance and results



Work Structure & Growth Path:

- Initial work will start with small tasks and project-based assignments (gig-based)

- This allows both sides to evaluate fit and working style

- Strong performance will quickly move into a part-time role (approximately 20 hours per week)

- From there, the role can grow into a full-time position (approximately 40 hours per week)



We are looking for someone interested in a long-term role — not short-term work.



Compensation:

- Starting benchmark: $5 USD per hour

- Part-time: ~20 hours per week

- Full-time: ~40 hours per week

- Consistent work available based on performance

- Opportunities for growth and increased responsibility over time



Work Setup:

- Flexible working hours

- Daily overlap required: between 8:00 AM – 10:00 AM Eastern Time (approximately 15-30 minutes per day, exact time coordinated with the team)

- Remaining work can be completed on your own schedule



Hiring Process:

- Short interview

- Paid test task ($15 USD, fixed price)



The test will be a small, real-world design task and should take approximately 30–60 minutes to complete.



How to Apply:



Please include:

- Your portfolio

- 2–3 examples of your strongest work

- What type of design you specialize in

- Include the word “CREATIVE” at the top of your application



Final Note:

We move fast and value people who take initiative, pay attention to detail, and take ownership of their work.



We are building a long-term team and are looking for reliable, consistent people who want to grow with us.



If that sounds like you, apply and let’s get started.

title: Virtual Executive Assistant — Accounting, Ecommerce & Marketing
description:
?? **IMPORTANT: Do NOT apply if you do not have an accounting degree or hands-on Xero experience. We will not consider applications without these. Please save your time and ours.**



The Role



We're looking for a rare kind of person. You studied accounting because you're logical, detail-oriented, and love systems. But you've always had a creative side that doesn't get used enough in a traditional accounting role. You thrive on ticking off tasks, shipping things fast, and seeing the direct impact of your work. And secretly, you've always wanted to design something that makes brands stick out in a crowded market.



If that's you, we want to talk.



About Us



We run five business entities across three countries. MXTology is a premium ready-to-drink cocktail brand that reached the Grand Final of Gordon Ramsay's Food Stars Australia — that's a core asset we lean on hard. Aussie Floats makes inflatable products for the Australian summer market. Floral Image is a corporate florist doing B2B and B2C deliveries. We also operate Floral Image in the USA and Canada.



Between all five entities, we're managing complex multi-jurisdictional accounting, multiple revenue streams, and aggressive growth targets across Australia, the USA, and Canada.



What You'll Actually Do



**Your primary focus right now is bookkeeping and financial operations across all five entities.**



You'll manage Xero as the single source of truth. Your core responsibilities include:



- Reconcile accounts across all five entities — Australian, US, and Canadian businesses

- Ensure full compliance with Australian GST, payroll, and superannuation requirements under Australian law

- Manage BAS lodgements, payroll processing, and super contributions for Australian staff

- Navigate US and Canadian tax and accounting requirements for our North American entities

- Manage inventory costing and reconciliation across MXTology and Aussie Floats

- Maintain the accounting calendar and make sure nothing falls through the cracks



This is where your accounting degree matters most. You need to understand multi-jurisdictional compliance, not just bookkeeping mechanics.



**But here's the critical piece — we're actively automating the accounting side of the business.** 

We're integrating AI tools, automating reconciliation workflows, and building systems so that the repetitive bookkeeping work becomes more hands-off over time. This isn't theoretical. We're investing in this now.



What that means for you is simple: as the accounting side gets automated, you transition into what you actually want to do.



You'll own Shopify operations for MXTology and Aussie Floats — updating product pages, optimizing conversions, managing inventory feeds, integrating apps. We use Klaviyo across all three consumer-facing businesses, so you'll manage
 sequences, customer data, and campaign execution. We use no-code tools — Bubble, Google Apps Script, Zapier — so you're orchestrating, not coding.



And then there's the creative work. You'll design social media assets, write copy for campaigns, help us position our brands as premium and intentional in markets full of noise. As we automate the accounting, this becomes your primary focus.



You'll work alongside the founder day to day, who handles big-picture strategy and vision. You execute with precision and speed.



Who We're Looking For



**Non-negotiable requirements:**

- Accounting degree (Bachelor's in Accounting, Finance, or equivalent)

- Hands-on Xero experience (not just "familiar with" — you've actually reconciled accounts, coded transactions, and run reports)

- Strong written English

- Reliable internet connection



**Strongly preferred:**

- Multi-jurisdictional accounting experience (Australia, USA, Canada, or any combination)

- Understanding of Australian GST, payroll, and superannuation compliance

- Shopify and/or Klaviyo experience

- AI fluency — you actively use Claude, ChatGPT, Midjourney, or similar tools in your daily workflow

- Creative ability — you can design social media posts, marketing
s, or brand assets that look professional and on-brand (Canva proficiency is a plus)

- Experience with no-code tools (Bubble, Zapier, Google Apps Script)



**The kind of person who thrives here:**

- You're fast — you'd rather ship something good today than perfect next month

- You love ticking off tasks and clearing a to-do list

- You're creative enough to design a social post that stops the scroll, but disciplined enough to reconcile a multi-entity balance sheet without cutting corners

- You see this role as a pathway — the accounting work is stable income and the foundation, but you're excited about the creative and marketing side growing as we automate the bookkeeping

- You're building something with us, not just doing a job



The Offer



Full-time: 40 hours per week. Ongoing role with clear growth pathway.



**Months 1–3 (Probationary Period): AUD $12 per hour**

- Annual equivalent: AUD $23,040

- This period is about proving execution and fit

- You'll get familiar with our systems, three countries, five entities, and our automation roadmap



**Month 4+ (Confirmed Role): AUD $15 per hour**

- Annual equivalent: AUD $30,000

- Performance-based increase — you've proven yourself

- We're investing in your longevity and growth

- This is when we really start building out automation and you transition toward more creative work



You'll work directly with the founder day to day. We iterate fast. And as we build out automation, you're part of designing what that looks like and how your role evolves.



How to Apply



**?? To confirm you've read this entire ad, start your application with the word "FIREBALL". Applications that don't start with this word will not be reviewed.**



Then tell us:



1. **Your accounting qualifications** — what degree do you hold, and any certifications (Xero, CPA, etc.)?



2. **Your Xero experience** — how many years, what types of entities have you managed, and have you done any multi-jurisdictional work (Australia, USA, Canada, or other)?



3. **Your AI experience** — give us ONE specific example of how you've used AI (Claude, ChatGPT, Midjourney, etc.) to complete a real work task faster or better. Be specific — generic answers like "I use ChatGPT sometimes" won't cut it.



4. **Your creative side** — show us ONE thing you've designed or created that you're proud of. A social media post, an
 campaign, a landing page, a brand asset — anything. Include a link or attach an image.



5. **Your availability** — confirm you can work 40 hours per week and let us know your timezone.



**All five questions must be answered in full. Incomplete applications will not be considered.**



We review applications within 48 hours. Shortlisted candidates will receive a short paid test task covering both a bookkeeping exercise and a Shopify/marketing execution task.



We move fast. If you're the right fit, you could be working with us within two weeks.





MXTology — Premium cocktails in a pouch. Grand Finalists on Gordon Ramsay's Food Stars Australia.

Aussie Floats — Inflatable fun for the Australian summer.

Floral Image — Corporate floristry for the ACT, Seattle and Vancouver

title: Supply Chain Coordinator
description:
We're hiring a Supply Chain Coordinator  to own the entire operational backbone of a growing Asian American art and lifestyle brand scaling toward $1M in revenue this year.



We sell original watercolor art-printed products — tote bags, tea towels, Swedish dish cloths, art prints — and our flagship product, the Ultimate Journal Bag. All products are manufactured in China and fulfilled through our own warehouse. The founder runs product development and content. Your job is to make sure everything else runs without her.



This means supplier relationships, inventory systems, financial tracking, warehouse coordination, and operational infrastructure — built, owned, and continuously improved by you. Not supported by you. Not assisted by you. Owned by you.

We are not looking for someone to maintain existing systems. We are looking for someone who builds them, then runs them, then makes them better — without being asked.



If your best work happens when someone tells you exactly what to do, this is the wrong role. If you have built operational infrastructure from scratch, caught problems before they became crises, and can show us the specific outcomes — keep reading!



Responsibilities

• Own all communication with China-based manufacturers. You will be the main person in charge of ensuring every product goes from design, production, and shipped to our warehouse. 

Track every active shipment from departure in China through US customs and final delivery to the warehouse. 

• Own inventory health across all SKUs. Build and maintain a reorder system with calculated reorder points based on sell-through rates and 45–70 day lead times. 

• Own the financial record-keeping of the business. 

• Ensure the warehouse team has everything they need to operate. Build and maintain SOPs for all warehouse function. 

• Own and maintain all operational boards in 
. Build automations that reduce manual work. Identify broken workflows and fix them before being told they are broken. 

• Use Claude and ChatGPT daily as core operational tools. Build a prompt library for recurring tasks. Proactively surface new AI tools and workflows that improve operations.



Requirements

• Minimum 3 years of experience in operations, supply chain, or business management within an e-commerce or product-based business.

• Demonstrated experience building operational systems from scratch — not inheriting and maintaining, but designing and implementing.

• Experience managing international supplier relationships. Chinese manufacturer experience and WeChat familiarity are strong advantages.

• Strong financial literacy — COGS, landed cost, gross margin, cash flow, and basic P&L concepts. You can build a financial summary a founder can actually use.

• Hands-on experience with invoicing, bookkeeping, and expense tracking. QuickBooks or Wave experience preferred.

• Advanced Google Sheets skills. You can build a working inventory tracker or COGS calculator from scratch.

• Proficient in 
 or a comparable project management platform.

• Actively uses AI tools — Claude, ChatGPT, or similar — as a core part of daily work. You write specific prompts, iterate on outputs, and use AI to build systems and solve problems. You will be asked to demonstrate this during the application process.

• Strong written English. You can represent the business professionally in all vendor correspondence.

• You have a specific example of identifying an operational problem before being told about it and solving it. You will be asked to share this during the application process.



Tools


, Google Sheets and Docs, Shopify, QuickBooks or Wave, Alibaba, WeChat, Claude or ChatGPT.



Results

• Founder has not sent a single message to a Chinese supplier within 30 days.

• Inventory reorder system live within 30 days — reorder points calculated, alerts active, zero stockouts going forward.

• COGS tracker complete for every SKU within 60 days.

• Monthly operational review delivered to founder proactively — not when asked.

title: SEO Link Builder (Outreach Specialist)
description:
We are seeking a highly skilled and proactive SEO Link Builder who can consistently acquire high-quality backlinks using a range of proven link building strategies. This role requires someone who understands both free (organic outreach) and paid (placement-based) link acquisition methods and knows how to apply them strategically to drive rankings and organic growth.



Key Responsibilities:



Plan and execute scalable link building campaigns across multiple strategies

Build high-quality backlinks using a mix of free and paid methods, including outreach, partnerships, and placements

Consistently deliver up to 30 new quality backlinks per month per website, while maintaining strict quality standards

Manage and execute link building campaigns across multiple websites simultaneously

Prospect, qualify, and prioritise backlink opportunities based on SEO impact

Conduct personalised outreach to website owners, editors, and publishers

Negotiate placements where required while maintaining quality standards

Perform competitor backlink analysis to identify gaps and opportunities

Maintain outreach pipelines and manage relationships with publishers

Track, measure, and report on link performance (rankings, traffic impact, indexation)



Core Link Building Strategies You Must Be Proficient In:



Guest posting (manual outreach and placements)

Niche edits / link insertions

Broken link building

Moving Man Method

Resource page link building

Digital PR / HARO-style outreach

Competitor backlink replication



Backlink Qualification Criteria:

You must demonstrate a strong ability to evaluate link opportunities using the following metrics:



Relevance

Strong topical alignment with our niche

Contextual relevance of the linking page (not just the domain)

Authority

Domain Rating (DR) / Domain Authority (DA)

Referring domains profile

Organic keyword footprint (not just DR as a vanity metric)

Traffic Quality

Consistent organic traffic (via Ahrefs/SEMrush)

Keywords ranking in relevant niches

Avoid sites with inflated or irrelevant traffic sources

Outbound Link Profile

Ratio of outbound links to content

No spammy or irrelevant casino/adult/low-quality links

Editorial standards of the site

Indexation & Visibility

Pages are indexed in Google

Site is actively ranking and not deindexed or penalised

Recent content is being crawled and ranked



Required Skills and Experience:



Proven experience building high-quality backlinks that improve rankings

Demonstrated ability to consistently deliver up to 30 quality backlinks per month per website

Proven ability to manage link building across multiple websites at the same time

Strong understanding of both white-hat and grey-hat link building approaches

Ability to scale outreach while maintaining link quality

Experience with tools such as Ahrefs, SEMrush, Hunter, Pitchbox, or similar

Strong written communication for outreach and negotiation

Ability to identify low-quality or risky links before acquisition



Application Requirement (Mandatory):

As part of your application, you must submit a 3–5 minute Loom video explaining the Moving Man Method.



Your video must clearly cover:



What the Moving Man Method is

How to find link opportunities (step-by-step)

Tools you would use (e.g., Ahrefs, Google search operators)

How you would structure your outreach message

Why this method works and when to use it



Applications without a Loom video will not be considered.

title: Part-Time Video Editor — Food & Travel Content (Ongoing)  About the role
description:
About the role



I'm a self-published author and content creator based in Southeast Asia producing short-form food and travel videos for YouTube, Instagram, and TikTok. I need a reliable video editor who can work from a script and shot notes to assemble polished, on-brand videos quickly. This starts as a part-time arrangement with strong potential for ongoing long-term work.



What you'll be doing



—

Assemble raw footage into finished videos using a provided script and shot-by-shot notes

—

Match footage to narrative beats (food close-ups, establishing shots, reaction 
nts)

—

Add captions, basic color grade, music sync, and any provided b-roll

—

Deliver exports in specified formats (YouTube 16:9, Reels/TikTok 9:16, sometimes both)

—

Follow a consistent template — once you learn it, you'll own it

What I'm looking for

—

Comfortable working from a written script with timestamped shot directions

—

—

Samples of past food, travel, or lifestyle video edits (required)

Volume & pay



Starting at 4–6 videos per month, scaling with results. Paid hourly — please state your rate when you apply. I'm looking for a long-term working relationship, not one-off projects. Editors who deliver clean work on deadline get more volume.



How to apply

Applications without samples will not be reviewed.

title: Operations Director
description:
We're hiring a Supply Chain Coordinator  to own the entire operational backbone of a growing Asian American art and lifestyle brand scaling toward $1M in revenue this year.



We sell original watercolor art-printed products — tote bags, tea towels, Swedish dish cloths, art prints — and our flagship product, the Ultimate Journal Bag. All products are manufactured in China and fulfilled through our own warehouse. The founder runs product development and content. Your job is to make sure everything else runs without her.



This means supplier relationships, inventory systems, financial tracking, warehouse coordination, and operational infrastructure — built, owned, and continuously improved by you. Not supported by you. Not assisted by you. Owned by you.

We are not looking for someone to maintain existing systems. We are looking for someone who builds them, then runs them, then makes them better — without being asked.



If your best work happens when someone tells you exactly what to do, this is the wrong role. If you have built operational infrastructure from scratch, caught problems before they became crises, and can show us the specific outcomes — keep reading!



Responsibilities

• Own all communication with China-based manufacturers. You will be the main person in charge of ensuring every product goes from design, production, and shipped to our warehouse. 

Track every active shipment from departure in China through US customs and final delivery to the warehouse. 

• Own inventory health across all SKUs. Build and maintain a reorder system with calculated reorder points based on sell-through rates and 45–70 day lead times. 

• Own the financial record-keeping of the business. 

• Ensure the warehouse team has everything they need to operate. Build and maintain SOPs for all warehouse function. 

• Own and maintain all operational boards in 
. Build automations that reduce manual work. Identify broken workflows and fix them before being told they are broken. 

• Use Claude and ChatGPT daily as core operational tools. Build a prompt library for recurring tasks. Proactively surface new AI tools and workflows that improve operations.



Requirements

• Minimum 3 years of experience in operations, supply chain, or business management within an e-commerce or product-based business.

• Demonstrated experience building operational systems from scratch — not inheriting and maintaining, but designing and implementing.

• Experience managing international supplier relationships. Chinese manufacturer experience and WeChat familiarity are strong advantages.

• Strong financial literacy — COGS, landed cost, gross margin, cash flow, and basic P&L concepts. You can build a financial summary a founder can actually use.

• Hands-on experience with invoicing, bookkeeping, and expense tracking. QuickBooks or Wave experience preferred.

• Advanced Google Sheets skills. You can build a working inventory tracker or COGS calculator from scratch.

• Proficient in 
 or a comparable project management platform.

• Actively uses AI tools — Claude, ChatGPT, or similar — as a core part of daily work. You write specific prompts, iterate on outputs, and use AI to build systems and solve problems. You will be asked to demonstrate this during the application process.

• Strong written English. You can represent the business professionally in all vendor correspondence.

• You have a specific example of identifying an operational problem before being told about it and solving it. You will be asked to share this during the application process.



Tools


, Google Sheets and Docs, Shopify, QuickBooks or Wave, Alibaba, WeChat, Claude or ChatGPT.



Results

• Founder has not sent a single message to a Chinese supplier within 30 days.

• Inventory reorder system live within 30 days — reorder points calculated, alerts active, zero stockouts going forward.

• COGS tracker complete for every SKU within 60 days.

• Monthly operational review delivered to founder proactively — not when asked.

• At least two workflow improvements proposed and implemented within 60 days without being asked.

• Zero stockouts due to missed reorder windows at 90 days.

• Payroll and tax filing support running on schedule with zero founder involvement at 90 days.

• Founder completely removed from day-to-day operational execution at 90 days.



Compensation

• $10–$15 USD per hour based on experience and demonstrated results.

• 15–20 hours per week to start. Strong potential to grow into a full-time role as the business scales. Performance-based rate increase available at 90-day review.

• Fully remote. Philippines-based candidates strongly encouraged to apply.



Our Values

• Ownership over execution. We don't want someone who completes tasks. We want someone who owns outcomes. The difference is whether you're thinking about results or just checking boxes.

• Proactive over reactive. If you wait to be told there's a problem, you're already behind. The right person for this role spots gaps, flags them early, and brings solutions — not just updates.

• Community first. Our customers feel like they know us because they do. We treat that relationship as the most valuable thing we have.

title: Operations Coordinator — Ministry of Growth

$900–$1,200/month | Full-time | Remote | Philippines-based | HIRING ASAP



? CEO interview on April 3 (Friday). No Loom video = no interview. Apply now.



ABOUT MINISTRY OF GROWTH

Ministry of Growth is not an agency. Not a consultancy. Not another vendor that charges a retainer, sends a monthly report, and moves on. We are an embedded growth partner. We go inside creator businesses, build the revenue engine, and operate it alongside them. Our model is simple: we only win when they win. Revenue share means our incentives are locked to your results. Always.



THE ROLE

You'll be the person who builds and maintains every client's system. When a new client comes on, you set up everything in GoHighLevel — the CRM, the automations, the pipelines, the
 sequences, the booking flows. You make sure it all works. Every day, you check that nothing is broken. If something breaks, you fix it before anyone notices.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

- Build GoHighLevel sub-accounts from scratch for every new client — pipelines, stages, custom fields, tags, automations

- Configure every automation: lead capture ? nurture sequences ? booking confirmations ? show-up reminders (24hr, 2hr, 15min) ? no-show recovery ? post-call follow-up ? won/lost flows

- Take copy from our Copy Strategist and wire it into GHL
/SMS sequences with proper triggers, delays, and conditions

- Set up calendars, intake forms, contracts, and payment integrations per client

- Create Slack channels and Notion project pages for each new client

- Test every automation end-to-end before launch — every form, every trigger, every
, every booking link

- Monitor daily: automations firing,
s delivering, booking flows working, nothing broken

- Update SOPs when processes change — documentation is part of your job



WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

Every client's funnel runs on automations. If a lead signs up and doesn't get the nurture sequence, we lose them. If show-up reminders don't fire, the prospect doesn't show up. If the booking flow is broken, we're paying for leads that go nowhere. You're the person who makes sure the machine runs. Without you, leads leak silently and nobody notices until revenue drops.



MUST-HAVES

- GoHighLevel experience — non-negotiable. You should know pipelines, automations, workflows, triggers, calendars, and forms.

- Experience with CRM automation (Zapier, Make, or similar is a plus)

- Notion proficiency — you'll manage our project workspace

NICE TO HAVE

- Experience at a marketing agency or with multiple client accounts

- Familiarity with Slack workflows and channel management

- Basic understanding of funnels, landing pages, and
 marketing



WHAT WE OFFER

- $900–$1,200/month — above market for PH, paid consistently

- Long-term role — we're building a 10+ client agency, you'll grow with us

- Clear SOPs — you'll never be guessing what to do

- Small team where your work directly impacts revenue

- Path to a senior operations role as we scale



HOW TO APPLY — FAST AND SIMPLE

title: YouTube Video Editor - Adventure Content - Gig Work
description:
I have a large archive of mountain biking and outdoor adventure footage organized and ready to edit. Need editor(s) for ongoing gig work.



FOOTAGE:

- Mountain bike races, POV, trails

- Skiing/snowboarding

- Hiking, nature walks, camping

- Travel, dining, coffee



DELIVERABLE:

- Maximum 7 day turnaround per video (we have a schedule for the year)

- Good pacing, some with a good music selection and others require no music

- Relatively simple edits, footage is often chronological and just needs the boring bits cut out



RATE:

Most projects will be one of two types of videos..

- $20-25: very simple POV footage supplied in chronological order, just needs to be trimmed > stitched together > add transitions > colour grade, usually about two hours of actual work (example here: 
 )

- $50-75: more varying footage organised into a VLOG-style presentation (example here: 
 ) requiring more time, putting things in order, music, etc.. expect these to be more like 10 hours of work



SYSTEM:

- I supply footage on Sunday night via dropbox link

- Your first draft is due on Wednesday night

- I review the draft and provide feedback by 12 noon Thursday

- Your final edit is due by midnight on Friday



For the rest of 2026, I want to release at least one simple POV riding video every week, and one VLOG every two weeks.  If I can find a reliable editor we might be able to do more :)



TRIAL:

There are three or four of the "very simple POV" videos above scheduled over the next month - we'll use those as trials and graduate to the VLOG-style footage if/when we're ready.



TO APPLY:

- Provide examples of action sport POV and VLOG-style videos you've created (PLEASE don't send me generic stuff, I want to see videos that are RELEVANT to my niche)

- Confirm you are comfortable with Davinci Resolve for editing and, most importantly, colour grading

title: GoHighLevel CRM & AI Automation Specialist
description:
I am a real estate investor looking to build a clean, scalable GoHighLevel system focused on automation, AI-driven lead nurturing, and efficient follow-up.



This is NOT a basic setup job - I am looking for someone who can think in systems, build smart workflows, and optimize for conversion.



Scope of work:

- Build and structure lead pipelines (New ? Contacted ? Warm ? Hot ? Offer ? Dead)

- Develop advanced workflows using triggers, conditions, and tagging logic

- Create SMS and
 sequences that adapt based on lead behavior (AI-style nurturing, not basic drip campaigns)

- Set up missed call text back, no-answer follow-ups, and multi-touch sequences

- Implement smart lead routing, tagging, and stage movement automatically

- Integrate AI elements where appropriate (Conversation AI, GPT, or conditional logic-based responses)

- Optional: webhook/Zapier integration with dialer (ReadyMode)



What I’m looking for:

- Strong experience with GoHighLevel automations and workflows

- Ability to build logic-based systems (if/then conditions, triggers, segmentation)

- Experience with AI-driven or dynamic lead nurturing (not just static campaigns)

- Understanding of lead generation and follow-up in real estate or similar industries



Goal:

Build a system that automates follow-up, increases response rates, and allows my team to operate efficiently without manual work.



Important:

- Keep the system clean, simple, and scalable (no over-engineering)

- Focus on performance and conversion, not complexity



To apply:

1. Send screenshots or a Loom video of workflows you’ve built

2. Briefly explain how you would approach AI-based lead nurturing inside GoHighLevel

3. Share your rate and estimated timeline



Looking to hire immediately for a fast turnaround.

title: Operations & Research Assistant
description:
Please review this job post completely before applying.



Job Title: Operations & Research Assistant

Type: Full-Time

Hours: 40 hrs/week (must overlap EST)

Location: Remote

Start Date: Immediate (within 1 week)



About the Role



We are a U.S.-based real estate investment company, but this role is focused on operations, research, and task support. You do not need real estate experience to succeed here.



We’re looking for a proactive, detail-oriented professional who can manage
, conduct web research, process assigned tasks, and support leadership with projects and decision-making. This is a role for someone resourceful and motivated — not a simple “calendar/email VA.” You’ll be given responsibility and trusted to deliver results with minimal supervision.



What You’ll Do



? Manage
 communication daily through Outlook (respond clearly and keep inbox organized)

? Conduct research on service providers (pricing, reviews, quality, terms) and prepare clear comparison presentations

? Process a variety of assigned tasks — from organizing data and documents to preparing reports and summaries

? Use task management tools (ClickUp, Asana, or similar) to stay organized and track deadlines

? Communicate updates and progress via Slack and other tools

? Join Zoom calls as needed to collaborate or share results

? Quickly learn new tools or processes by researching online or watching tutorials (YouTube, guides, etc.)

? Provide reliable support to leadership by ensuring tasks are completed accurately and on time



Must-Have Skills & Requirements



? 3+ years in an operations, executive assistant, or research-heavy support role

? Excellent English (written and spoken) — professional and clear communicator

? Strong web research skills — able to find, evaluate, and summarize information independently

? Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint)

? Familiarity with task management tools (ClickUp, Asana, Trello, or similar)

? Comfortable using Slack, Zoom, and ChatGPT for communication and task execution

? Highly detail-oriented, organized, and dependable without direct supervision

? Strong workstation setup: desk, chair, reliable desktop/laptop, 1–2 monitors, headset, and camera

? Positive energy, motivated mindset, and the ability to “vibe” well with the team



Nice to Have



? Experience preparing structured presentations (Google Slides, PowerPoint)

? Vendor research or procurement support background

? Prior remote work experience with structured task management systems



Why Work With Us



? Long-term, stable position with consistent hours

? Opportunity to take on diverse tasks and grow into higher-level responsibilities

? Professional team that values independence, results, and reliability

? Supportive work culture with room for personal contribution



How to Apply (Read Carefully)



We do not accept direct applications on OnlineJobs.ph.



Apply only using this link:







Only applications submitted via the above form will be reviewed.

title: Ecommerce Video Editor (Wellness Lifestyle Brand)
description:
We are a fast-growing e-commerce wellness brand. Our brand narrative is the "Return to Nature."



We are looking for a full-time Direct Response Video Editor. You will be the engine of our paid acquisition strategy on Meta and TikTok.



You are not just a splicer; you are a visual storyteller. You will receive raw product assets and scripts, but you should also know to source high-quality organic B-roll and leverage AI tools to generate conceptual visuals that fit our narrative.



What You Will Do:



Edit at Scale: Produce short-form direct response variations daily for Meta/TikTok testing.



Visual Storytelling: Weave together raw product footage with high-retention storytelling frameworks



Hook Mastery: Master the "First 3 Seconds" hook psychology to stop the scroll.



Native Formatting: Apply native-looking text overlays and captions that blend seamlessly into social feeds.



What We Do NOT Want (Please read carefully):



Do NOT apply if you only edit cinematic travel vlogs. We are a Direct Response brand. Every second must be designed to convert.



Requirements:



Proven track record editing Direct Response video ads that generate engagement/ views, or even better, profitable sales for e-commerce/DTC brands.



Deep understanding of pacing, storytelling, and viewer retention metrics.



Expert proficiency in CapCut, Premiere Pro, or Final Cut.



Experience prompting AI video/image generators to match a specific brand aesthetic.



How to Apply:

Please include a link to your portfolio, work, videos or ads you've created, and if you've gotten results for your previous works.

title: Administrative Assistant
description:
We are looking for a resourceful, high-output Administrative Assistant to serve as the operational right hand to our leadership team. This is not a passive support role. You will be directly involved in client and employee communications, proposal development, CRM management, data entry across multiple platforms, and daily coordination across every function of the business. You will touch nearly every tool in our tech stack and be expected to learn fast, work independently, and keep things moving.



Responsibilities

Client and Employee Communications



Draft, send, and manage
 correspondence with clients, prospects, employees, and vendors on behalf of leadership

Communicate directly with Account Managers and field staff regarding scheduling, onboarding, supply needs, and account updates



Handle inbound inquiries professionally and route them appropriately

Support client onboarding touchpoints including 24-hour, 7-day, and
day check-ins



Proposals, Documents, and Presentations



Assist in preparing client proposals, RFP responses, and service agreements using Qwilr, Google Docs, Canva, and Microsoft Word

Build and format pitch decks, one-pagers, onboarding packets, and internal documentation

Proofread, edit, and quality-check all outbound materials before delivery

Maintain organized templates and asset libraries for recurring deliverables



CRM, Data Entry, and Systems Management



Manage and update records in GoHighLevel including leads, pipeline stages, contact records, and campaign data

Enter and maintain data

Log and track sales activity, walkthrough submissions, and account status changes

Maintain clean, accurate data across all platforms -- no loose ends



Operations and Scheduling Support



Coordinate scheduling and staffing logistics

Assist with supply orders, vendor communication, and equipment tracking

Support onboarding documentation for new client accounts and new hires through BambooHR and internal checklists

Help manage background check submissions through C4 Operations



General Administrative Support



Manage leadership calendars, meeting scheduling, and follow-ups

Organize and maintain Google Drive file structures

Research vendors, gather quotes, and compile comparison documents

Handle ad hoc projects including report generation, data cleanup, and process documentation



Required Qualifications



At least 2 years of experience as a Virtual Assistant or Administrative Assistant in a fast-paced environment

Excellent written and spoken English -- you will be communicating directly with clients and employees

Proficiency in Google Workspace (Gmail, Docs, Sheets, Drive, Calendar)

Experience with CRM platforms (GoHighLevel or similar)

Highly organized with strong attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously

Reliable internet connection and a professional home office setup

Available to work US business hours (Eastern Time)



Compensation and Details



Full-time (40 hours/week)

Weekly Pay via PayPal



TO APPLY:



You MUST submit a resume AND voice note to be considered. No exceptions.

title: Print-on-Demand Design Creator
description:
Summary

I own a history-themed e-commerce apparel brand. We create intelligent, historically inspired designs that combine strong typography, clean composition, and subtle wit.



I am looking for a graphic designer to join our existing designers who can take a simple concept and turn it into a finished, production-ready design — without needing step-by-step art direction.



This role requires independent creative thinking.



If you rely heavily on AI to generate finished artwork, this will not be a good fit.



Using AI as a supplemental tool for research, texture creation, or idea exploration is completely fine. However, the final composition, typography decisions, layout, and overall design direction must be driven by you — not by AI image generation tools.



I am not looking for:



Midjourney prompt engineers



Canva template designers



Clip-art assemblers



Fully AI-generated art resold as original work



I am looking for:



A designer who understands composition



Someone strong in typography



Someone who can research historical subjects



Someone who can build a cohesive visual identity over time



What I Will Provide:



A concept (example: “Battle of Midway – minimalist WWII naval blueprint style”)



Access to my current designs for brand direction



Historical context when needed



Feedback focused on alignment — not micromanagement



What I Need From You:



Original design thinking



Strong typographic hierarchy



Apparel-aware composition



Clean vector or raster artwork built intentionally



Production-ready PNG files



Technical Requirements:



4500 x 5400 px



300 DPI



Transparent background



Print-ready for direct-to-garment



Also helpful if you understand:



Screen-print-friendly layouts



Distressed / vintage treatments



Blueprint or engraving-style illustration



Clean negative space usage

title: Creative Designer (Graphic Design + Video Editing)
description:
Job Title: Creative Designer (Graphic Design + Video Editing)

Location: Remote (Working Hours: 9 AM – 6 PM PST Monday to Friday)



Position Summary

We’re looking to grow our creative team with a versatile and highly skilled Creative Designer who excels in graphic design and video editing. If you’re passionate about creating engaging visual content and have an eye for detail, this is an exciting opportunity to work on high-impact projects for some of the biggest automotive brands in the world.

You’ll collaborate with our in-house U.S. creative team to bring concepts to life across digital channels from ad creatives and motion videos to social media content and beyond. If you also have experience with motion graphics, that’s a huge plus.



Key Responsibilities

? Design compelling static and animated creatives for digital ads, social media, and marketing materials

? Edit short and long-form videos for use across social media platforms like TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube, and internal communications

? Apply text overlays, music, transitions, and motion graphics to enhance visual storytelling

? Work with brand guidelines and ensure visual consistency across all assets

? Collaborate with copywriters, media buyers, and account managers to align visuals with marketing objectives

? Handle multiple creative requests with tight deadlines and high accuracy



Requirements

? At least 3 years of professional experience in graphic design and video editing

? Proficiency in design tools such as Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, After Effects, or similar

? Strong portfolio showcasing digital ad creatives, social posts, and video projects

? Experience creating content for social platforms like Meta, TikTok, and YouTube

? Solid understanding of layout, typography, color theory, and motion timing

? Ability to follow brand guidelines while bringing fresh creative ideas

? Strong organizational and communication skills

? Must be available to work 9 AM – 6 PM PST, Monday to Friday

? Reliable internet connection and a distraction-free remote setup



Nice to Have

? Experience with motion graphics and animation using Adobe After Effects

? Familiarity with Canva, Figma, or similar collaborative tools

? Knowledge of export settings, fi le formats, and optimizing content for various platforms

? Automotive or performance marketing creative experience



What We Offer

? Opportunity to work on campaigns for top automotive manufacturers

? Ongoing training and a chance to grow your skills alongside a professional U.S. creative team

? Overtime hours available based on workload and performance

? A supportive, team-first culture that values quality, speed, and innovation



Ready to create high-performing content seen by thousands of viewers daily?

Apply now and let’s build something amazing together.

title: Short-Form Video & Content Marketing VA

The founder is highly creative and produces a large volume of raw content but needs support turning that into consistent, strategic, revenue-driving digital assets.

This is an early-stage, self-funded brand with strong personality and significant growth potential.

Schedule and publish content across platforms

Help create consistency and 
ntum across TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube Shorts

Work Hours: Flexible, overlap with US CST preferred

nts

ntum, not perfection.

title: Creative Strategist & Video Editor (Business & Entrepreneurship)
description:
I am looking for a partner, not just an editor. I run a portfolio of active brick-and-mortar businesses (retail, wellness, hospitality, and real estate) and I want to start documenting the reality of managing them. I am not looking to be a "guru"—I want to share practical lessons, operations, and the actual day-to-day of running companies.



I need someone who can take raw ideas and footage and turn them into compelling stories for YouTube and short-form platforms (TikTok/Reels/Shorts).



What You Will Do

- Strategy & Ideation: You will help me extract ideas from my weekly experiences. We will have brief calls where I share what happened in the businesses, and you will help identify the best "hooks" and angles.

- Editing: You will edit high-quality, engaging videos. The style should be clean and professional, focusing on retention without being overly "hype-y."

- Workflow Management: You will help structure the content calendar so I can focus on operating the businesses while you handle the content pipeline.



Who You Are

- You understand the business content niche (you know what works on YouTube right now).

- You are excellent at storytelling—you know how to cut fluff and keep the viewer interested.

- You are independent. You don't need a script for everything; you can take a rough concept or a raw "brain dump" and structure it into a cohesive video.



To Apply Please send:

1. A link to your portfolio (specifically business or educational content).

2. One sentence on why you think most business content is boring.

3. Your monthly rate or per-video rate.

title: DATA / ETL ENGINEER
description:
We Don't Need a SQL Jockey. We Need an Engineer Who Turns Raw Data Into Business Decisions.

Data / ETL Engineer

Most data engineers move data around. You'll build the pipelines that tell us what's winning and what's not.



About Rescale Media

We're not just another e-commerce company. We're a brand incubator on a mission to build enduring, value-driven brands that bring meaningful products into families' lives.

We've scaled from just drop shipping to a 30+ person team doing $1M+/month. Our goal? Multi 9-figures. And we're just getting started.

Learn more at 
dia



Our Core Values

GENIUSES & OWNERS We partner with builders who think like founders. Not employees waiting to be told what to do—owners who see a problem and fix it.

ITERATIONS > HOURS We don't measure success by time at your desk. We measure it by how fast you can test, learn, and improve. Ship it. Learn. Repeat.

TRUTH OVER EGO We prioritize honesty and clarity, even when it's uncomfortable. No politics. No BS. Just data and results.



The Mission

Close the loop between execution and results.

You'll build the data systems that tell us what's working—connecting performance data to decisions so we can double down on winners and kill losers fast.



What You'll Do

Build and maintain performance data ingestion pipelines

Implement attribution logic to connect actions to outcomes

Create winner detection systems that surface top performers

Design and optimize incremental ETL pipelines

Ensure data quality and consistency across sources

Build dashboards and reporting for business decisions

What We're Looking For

2+ years of data engineering or ETL experience

Strong SQL skills and experience with data warehouses

Experience building incremental/streaming pipelines

Understanding of data modeling and schema design

Proficiency in Python

Familiarity with orchestration tools (Airflow, Dagster, etc.)



Bonus Points:

Experience with e-commerce or marketing data

Background with attribution modeling

You've built real-time or near-real-time pipelines
